items.title: Longtime Trail Blazers broadcaster Kevin Calabro parts ways with team due to 'subprime' offer
items.summary: The Portland Trail Blazers made headlines in May after the organization laid off about 70 people as part of a restructuring under new team owner Tom Dundon. The negative press kept coming Wednesday, when The Oregonian's Joe Freeman reported that longtime broadcaster Kevin Calabro was parting ways with the organization.Calabro wasn't the only one. He left under his own terms, but Forbes' Maury Brown also reported that "TV analyst Michael Holton and statistician Tom Haberstroh, as well as TV hosts Neil Everett and Jamie Hudson have all been let go."Portland released a statement about the cuts to its broadcast teams. "We are deeply grateful to all the hard-working individuals who have contributed to our award-winning broadcasts over the years. I can't comment on individual personnel decisions, but I can confirm that we are making changes for the upcoming…

items.title: Did Adam Silver's remarks delay LeBron James' free agency choice?
items.summary: There's a logical explanation to why it's taking so long for LeBron James to announce his free agency decision on where he will play in the 2026-27 season.A Cleveland sports radio host believes that James has intentionally pushed back his announcement because he was "irritated" with comments made last week by NBA Commissioner Adam Silver.Cleveland sports radio personality Andy Baskin said Silver's comments "irritated" James and the former Los Angeles Lakers star decided to push back the announcement. Baskin, who has a radio show called Baskin and Phelps on 92.3 The Fan, went as far as to say that James was ready to announce his decision last week."LeBron's crew was probably ready to make an announcement, especially because they were in New York," Baskin said. "The world had their eyes on New York because of the World Cup as well. Yeah,…

items.title: Grizzlies, D'Angelo Russell expected to work together to allow veteran guard to find new home
items.summary: Grizzlies point guard D'Angelo Russell is not believed to be a long-term piece in Memphis, with both sides expected to work together toward a resolution that would allow the 30-year-old to find a new home at some point this offseason — whether via a buyout agreement or a trade — sources tell Yahoo Sports.Russell, who appeared in just 25 games last season in Dallas, arrived in Memphis earlier this month as part of an expansive six-team deal involving Milwaukee, Washington, Dallas, the LA Clippers and Detroit. The trade also sent center Isaiah Stewart, guard AJ Johnson, three future second-round picks, a conditional first-round pick and a second-round swap to the Grizzlies.The veteran guard was assumed to be part of a ball-handling group in Dallas last season designed to assist in the development of then-rookie Cooper Flagg, but started just three games — seemingly falling out of…

items.title: The Indecision: What's LeBron James waiting for?
items.summary: LeBron James has never simply signed somewhere. In 2010, he joined the Heat because Dwyane Wade was already in place and they acquired Chris Bosh. In 2014, he returned home to Cleveland to join Kyrie Irving with the understanding that the first overall draft pick was getting flipped for Kevin Love. In 2018, he signed with the Lakers knowing Year 1 was a bridge to a blockbuster. The next summer, Anthony Davis arrived. Three free agency decisions all had one common thread: LeBron signed up for a team with a plan.This brings us to the summer of 2026. The popular theory around the league is that LeBron has been slow-playing his decision to see if any of his suitors could find a way to land Irving or Davis. If a team proved capable of acquiring one of his championship teammates, sources say, it might sway him in that direction.Nobody has been able or willing to do it yet. The Irving path…

The Dominican scorer had already proved his skills in the G League before signing with San Antonio but looked ready to…

items.title: Miami mistakenly post James 'introductory' video
items.summary: LeBron James is entering his 24th season in the NBA [Getty Images]Miami Heat mistakenly posted a link on YouTube to LeBron James' 'introductory press conference' despite the NBA great not yet deciding where he will play next season.The link to the live stream - scheduled for 27 July - was uploaded to the Heat's channel on Tuesday before being deleted.A Heat spokesperson told Reuters the video was posted in error by staff who were preparing for the possibility of James joining the side.BBC Sport has contacted Miami Heat for comment.James, 41, announced he was leaving the Los Angeles Lakers in June after eight years, in which he led them to the 2020 NBA title - his fourth overall.He previously played for Miami between 2010 and 2014.James averaged 20.9 points, 7.2 assists and 6.1 rebounds last season with the Lakers. The 22-time All-Star is the NBA's all-time leader in…

items.title: Kendrick Perkins convinced we got first ‘real big clue’ of LeBron James’ free agency
items.summary: LeBron JamesKendrick Perkins doesn’t believe it was a meaningless mistake.ESPN’s Kendrick Perkins said on “First Take” that the Miami Heat uploading a video on Tuesday night titled “LeBron James Introductory press conference” was no accident.“Those things don’t just happen under Pat Riley’s watch,” Perkins said. “… This means that Miami is really in the hunt. They’re in the thick of this. LeBron James could possibly end up back in South Beach.”LeBron James playing against the Knicks on Feb. 1, 2026. JASON SZENES/ NY POSTPerkins went on to say that this was the “first real big clue” of the offseason from an organization that James has interest in.On Wednesday morning, the Miami Herald reported that the deleted video, scheduled to air on Monday, July 27th, was accidentally uploaded by the Heat’s social media department as they were preparing for the possibility of James rejoining the…

items.title: Are the Heat odds-on favorites for LeBron James after YouTube debacle?
items.summary: LeBron James is 41 years old, will be 42 by the end of the 2026-27 NBA season, and yet his free agency still has NBA fans everywhere on the edge of their seats. Rumors have swirled, podcasts have had whole episodes dedicated to trying to figure out where The King will opt to play next season.On Tuesday night, if even for a moment, fans believed he'd made a decision. Fans discovered that the Miami Heat had scheduled a video to be published on YouTube titled "LeBron James Introductory Press Conference." Everyone around social media was flabbergasted at how sudden James' decision seemingly was. Turns out, it was an accident.The Miami Heat accidentally posted, then deleted, a scheduled YouTube livestream titled “LeBron James Introductory Press Conference” for July 27.A team spokesperson told @ShamsCharania the link was mistakenly published while preparing for a possible…

items.title: Winnipeg Jets poke fun at Miami Heat YouTube blunder as LeBron free agency continues
items.summary: The Winnipeg Jets keep the heat up on Miami’s Lebron James slip up.With Lebron free agency rumors still circling, the Heat accidentally uploaded a page titled “LeBron James Introductory Press Conference” to their YouTube.The post was scheduled for July 27 and was quickly taken down, but NBA fans pounced on the blunder and it was soon viral.The Winnipeg Jets poked fun at the Miami Heat accidental YouTube post on X. @NHLJets/YouTubeThe Heat have since said the upload was a mistake, and they were only preparing for a potential reunion with James, but it nonetheless changed the odds of his landing spot.The Jets responded to the debacle with an X post that read “The Winnipeg Jets’s official YouTube channel has LeBron’s introductory press conference scheduled for July 27.”The caption was accompanied with a link to a real scheduled live stream from the Jets main YouTube account titled “LeBron…

items.title: NBA Offseason Trade/Free Agent Rumors 2026: Mario Hezonja return rumors, plus Bradley Beal, Anthony Davis
items.summary: While the NBA world impatiently waits — and a lot of free agent action is held up — until LeBron James announces his decision on his next team, rumors are still flying around. Here is some of the latest free agency chatter around the Association.Mario Hezonja drawing interestThe last time we saw Mario Hezonja in the NBA was with Portland during the bubble, a season where he came off the bench and averaged 4.8 points per game. Since then, the former No. 5 pick has played and starred for European powerhouse Real Madrid, but now there is buzz about an NBA return at age 11. Marc Stein of The Stein Line has been in front of this story.Latest on former No. 5 overall pick Mario Hezonja per league sources: Hezonja indeed expected to rejoin the NBA for the first time since 2019-20. Multiple NBA teams have shown interest in the Real Madrid forward. List said to include teams also pursuing LeBron…

items.title: There’s a method to the Mavericks madness this offseason
items.summary: NEW YORK, NEW YORK - JULY 17: Winner Cooper Flagg poses for a photo after Call Of Duty: Modern Warfare 4 Grudge Match with Jeremiyah Love at Fanatics Fest at Javits Center on July 17, 2026 in New York City. (Photo by Manny Carabel/Getty Images for Activision/Call of Duty) | Getty Images for Activision/Call One year after selecting Cooper Flagg with the number one overall pick, the Dallas Mavericks are still trying to find a forward that will fit alongside him.Having a strong pair on the wing is important for roster construction in today’s NBA. The Mavs will need to toy with all of the new pieces that have been brought to the puzzle.“The three most notable additions here all fall into that big wing category: Morez Johnson with the No. 9 overall pick, Santi Aldama in a trade with Memphis and Zaccharie Risacher in a trade with Atlanta. Is the fit here optimal? Probably…
